AMZN.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on April 30 (after close) — 1 trading day away. This is a high-impact binary event with consensus at $177.3B revenue and $1.67 EPS (near the high end of Amazon's own $173.5B-$178.5B guidance range, meaning the bar is already set for an upside beat). FOMC rate decision also falls today (April 29), adding additional event risk. Net news sentiment is strongly positive: AWS AI ARR at $15B disclosed in Jassy shareholder letter, $11.6B Globalstar acquisition closed, AWS Trainium3 launched (3x faster AI training chip), Amazon Pharmacy GLP-1 same-day delivery partnership with Eli Lilly, and 64/68 analyst Buy ratings with average price target $284-287. Insider activity: quiet — no insider trades in the last 30 days. Technical setup going in: POOR and overextended. Daily RSI-14 at 86.14, RSI-21 at 89.35 — extreme overbought territory. Price is 17.46% above the 50-SMA and 30.28% higher over the past 21 days (z-score 2.0). The stock is already pricing in a strong beat with no margin of safety. Hourly indicators are distinctly deteriorating: OBV is diverging bearishly from price (distribution signal), hourly RSI divergence is bearish, MACD histogram is negative (-0.48), and minus_di (27.61) exceeds plus_di (18.37) on the hourly ADX — intraday momentum has flipped bearish. The catalyst is real and imminent, but the risk/reward for a new long entry at these levels is unfavorable. The stock has already run 13-14% on the prior catalyst cluster (Globalstar, Jassy letter, Trainium3). Entering 1 day before earnings at RSI 86 means any miss or guidance soft-patch from AWS triggers severe reversion from extreme levels. The post-earnings setup (if results beat) would be more constructive. Output: neutral — catalyst exists but technical setup is exhausted, not constructive.
